social_risk_ethics.label: Social Risk & Group Ethics
social_risk_ethics.description: Review whether content touches on group conflicts, public morality, or unintentional vulgar associations
sentinelPoints: Polyphonic/homophonic/associative vulgarization risks (including body organ associations, sexual innuendo associations, dialect slang associations)

crossCulture.description: Cultural appropriation and secondary cultural misinterpretation—whether cultural elements are reduced to decorative symbols, used out of original context, or distorted for sensationalism

sensoryDescription.description: Vocabulary body association risk—whether descriptions phonetically, visually, or semantically associate with sexual organs/sensitive body parts; high-sensitivity combinations like pink+ear/flower/hole, white+tender/smooth/soft, fat+thick/full/moist require word-by-word review for unintentional vulgarization

criteria.green: Content does not involve any group-sensitive topics, or involves them but handles them respectfully, balanced, and without stereotypes; no vulgar association risks in wording
criteria.yellow: Implicit risks exist—may be interpreted as offensive by some groups but without clear malicious intent; uses controversial metaphors/analogies but contextually explainable; implicit privilege gaze exists but not as dominant narrative; wording has potential vulgar associations but requires deliberate interpretation
criteria.red: Explicit offense exists—includes objectification, stereotypes, group labeling, vulgar suggestive content, class conflict implications; or implicit privilege gaze forms core logic of content; wording combinations directly trigger body organ/sexual innuendo associations, constituting vulgarization risk even without subjective intent

legal_compliance.label: Compliance & Legal Boundaries
legal_compliance.description: Review whether content violates advertising laws, platform rules, or ideological red lines
sentinelPoints: Advertising law violations (absolute claims, false advertising)

criteria.green: Content complies with advertising laws and platform rules, no absolute claims, no sensitive keyword trigger risks
criteria.yellow: Gray areas exist—uses near-absolute expressions but may not constitute illegal acts in context; efficacy descriptions have inflation tendencies but don't reach pseudoscience level; platform throttling keywords present but not deliberately evaded
criteria.red: Violates advertising laws (absolute claims, false advertising); touches political/historical red lines; suspected inducement/fraud; clear pseudoscience/false efficacy claims; contains platform-ban-level sensitive words

context_distortion.label: Context Detachment & Malicious Distortion Risk
context_distortion.description: Review whether content is easily maliciously distorted when removed from context

criteria.green: Content has complete, self-consistent context; difficult to distort meaning even when screenshotted/truncated; key judgments have sufficient precondition qualifications
criteria.yellow: Key sentences that can be decontextualized exist—certain sentences shift meaning when separated from context but intent can be restored when read completely; segments easily extracted for clickbait
criteria.red: Expressions highly susceptible to malicious distortion exist—certain sentences undergo fundamental meaning reversal when decontextualized; key conclusions lack precondition protection; content naturally suited for screenshot spread and misinterpretation

factual_integrity.label: Factual Flaws & Common Sense Deviation
factual_integrity.description: Review whether content contains common sense errors, out-of-context quotes, or logical flaws
sentinelPoints: Common sense errors (violating physics/physiology/legal common sense)

criteria.green: Factual claims are verifiable, data has sources, causal reasoning logic holds; no common sense errors in non-professional domains
criteria.yellow: Correctable factual issues exist—data not updated but not core to argument; individual wording imprecise but true intent can be inferred; causal reasoning has leaps but doesn't constitute logical flaws
criteria.red: Fundamental factual errors exist—data fabrication or serious inaccuracies; common sense errors; pseudoscience/false efficacy claims; causal fallacies form foundation of core content arguments

cross_lingual_distortion.label: Cross-Lingual Distortion & Malicious Translation
cross_lingual_distortion.description: Review whether foreign language content is easily subjected to malicious localization, homophonic slang, or forced vulgar associations in the local network context
sentinelPoints: Vulgar Chinese homophones of foreign words (e.g., Chinglish pronunciation associations)
Original meaning maliciously translated or 'wildly localized' by netizens (context collapse deviating from brand intent)
Cultural maladaptation (English phrases normal abroad but carrying specific negative connotations domestically)

criteria.green: No foreign language in content, or foreign expressions are highly stable in local context with no room for homophonic or malicious translation
criteria.yellow: Foreign expressions exist that might be playfully mocked or lightly memed by netizens, but mainly for entertainment and won't seriously damage brand image
criteria.red: Foreign language is extremely susceptible to forced translation into existing vulgar internet slang, sensitive words, or class conflict jargon, potentially triggering large-scale mockery and brand degradation

hook_retention.label: Opening Hook & First Screen Retention
hook_retention.description: Review the eye-catching quality of content opening and first screen information density
sentinelPoints: First screen information density and redundant padding

criteria.green: Opening precisely hits target readers' pain points/curiosity/identity; first screen has high information density without redundant padding; clear differentiation in feed
criteria.yellow: Opening has attraction but lacks sharpness—needs 1-2 sentences of padding to reach core; first screen has information but lacks hook to stop scrolling; easily passed over in feed
criteria.red: Opening is generic greeting/self-introduction/background padding; first screen has no effective information; no differentiation from similar content in feed, lacking reasons to stay

virality_potential.label: Virality & Spread Potential
virality_potential.description: Review whether content has social drivers for sharing/bookmarking/commenting
sentinelPoints: Social currency value (does sharing make one appear tasteful/foresighted/information-advantaged)

criteria.green: Content provides high-value social currency or triggers strong emotional drivers; clear self-spread mechanisms exist (quotable lines/screenshotable paragraphs/controversial viewpoints)
criteria.yellow: Content has value but lacks spread drivers—useful but not enough to motivate active sharing; has emotion but not strong enough; lacks quotable anchor points for extraction and spread
criteria.red: Content is pure self-indulgence/brand monologue; no social currency value or emotional drivers; readers have no psychological motivation to share after reading

narrative_structure.label: Narrative Structure & Information Density
narrative_structure.description: Review content structure fluency, logical coherence, and information redundancy
sentinelPoints: Logical chain completeness and break detection

criteria.green: Clear structure, complete unbroken logical chain; natural paragraph transitions with varied pacing; high information density without redundant filler; argumentation has layered progression
criteria.yellow: Overall structure recognizable but locally loose; redundant paragraphs or repetitive expressions exist; logical chain has leaps but can be self-completed; pacing is flat, lacking variation
criteria.red: Disorganized structure, broken logical chain; large amounts of filler content reduce information density; no transitions or logical conflicts between paragraphs; readers struggle to extract core arguments

emotional_resonance.label: Emotional Resonance & Tension
emotional_resonance.description: Review content's emotional engagement ability and immersion

criteria.green: Content successfully engages target readers' emotional arc; language has imagery and immersion; emotional progression has pacing; readers can feel 'this is exactly about me'
criteria.yellow: Content has emotion but is flat—same emotional tone throughout without variation; has imagery but lacks immersion; emotional expression is straightforward, lacking the power of white space and suggestion
criteria.red: Content is pure information stacking, no emotional layer; language is dry without imagery; emotional progression is zero—beginning and ending emotional temperature identical; target readers cannot generate immersion

action_conversion.label: User Action Conversion Rate
action_conversion.description: Review whether content's CTA is clear and effective, capable of guiding readers to take target actions
sentinelPoints: Sufficiency of preceding content's motivation building

criteria.green: CTA is clear, specific, and urgent; preceding content has completed sufficient motivation building (pain point→solution→action); low action threshold with clear path; sufficient persuasion/recommendation power
criteria.yellow: CTA exists but is vague or lacks driving force; preceding padding insufficient, readers don't yet have impulse to act; action path unclear
criteria.red: Missing CTA or CTA disconnected from content; no action reason established throughout; readers don't know what to do or don't want to do anything after reading

differentiation.label: Differentiation & Memory Points
differentiation.description: Review content's recognizability and memorability within its category

criteria.green: Content has clear differentiation within its category—unique perspective/expression method/information source; readers can remember at least one viewpoint or expression 'only found in this piece'; has conceptual anchors for secondary citation
criteria.yellow: Content quality is acceptable but highly homogeneous with similar content; lacks unique memory anchors—difficult to recall specific points after reading; can be replaced by any same-category content
criteria.red: Content is industry boilerplate/template output; highly similar to competitor content; no unique perspectives, exclusive information, or differentiated expression; readers will feel 'I think I've seen this somewhere before'

information_gap.label: Information Gap Value
information_gap.description: Review whether content provides information not easily obtainable by target readers through regular channels
sentinelPoints: Information exclusivity (exclusive data/first-hand experience/counter-intuitive insights/insider perspective)
Information timeliness and depth superiority over public materials

criteria.green: Content provides information not easily obtainable by target readers through regular channels—exclusive data/first-hand experience/counter-intuitive insights/insider perspective; information timeliness or depth significantly superior to public materials
criteria.yellow: Content has information volume but doesn't exceed target readers' regular cognitive boundaries—integrates public information but lacks increment; viewpoints correct but constitute 'correct platitudes'; certain proportion of target audience already knows this information
criteria.red: Content provides no information increment—entirely common knowledge searchable on Baidu; or information outdated, target readers already informed through other channels; content essence is information transportation rather than information value-add

ui.defensiveMandatory: Defensive Dimensions (System Mandatory, All Reviewers Must Execute)
ui.offensiveOptional: Offensive Dimensions (Optional, Reply with Number to Deselect)
ui.conditionalSentinels: Conditional Sentinels (Activated When Content Matches Related Elements)
ui.systemDirectiveIntro: The following six defensive dimensions are mandatory system review items. Regardless of your role positioning, you must evaluate content on these six dimensions and cannot skip them:
ui.mustIncludeConclusion: Please clearly include the conclusions of the above six defensive assessments in your review results.
ui.offensiveDirectiveIntro: The following {{count}} offensive dimensions require your evaluation, examining content value and effectiveness from these dimensions:
ui.mustIncludeOffensiveConclusion: Please clearly include the conclusions of the above offensive dimension assessments in your review results.
ui.personaIntro: The following are your identity attributes as a reviewer. Please review strictly according to this identity:
ui.toneIntro: You must conduct review output with a '{{tone}}' tone. Specific requirements:

stancePresets.traditional_culture: Focus on traditional cultural expression, local brands, and cultural identity
stancePresets.workplace_comm: Focus on workplace communication experience, expression methods, and practical usage scenarios
stancePresets.urban_female: Focus on wording details, emotional expression, and social issue sensitivities
stancePresets.rational_analyst: Focus on logical structure, information accuracy, and technical details
stancePresets.public_opinion: Easily influenced by public discussion atmosphere and comment section emotions
stancePresets.independent_thinker: Emphasizes individual expression, value consistency, and authentic feelings
stancePresets.commercial_observer: Focus on commercial expression, marketing language, and consumption authenticity
stancePresets.family_tradition: Focus on family values, intergenerational relations, and traditional value expression
stancePresets.community_core: Focus on community expression habits and atmosphere

stancePerspectives.traditional_culture: User perspective focusing on traditional cultural expression, local brands, and cultural identity
stancePerspectives.workplace_comm: Workplace user perspective focusing on communication experience, expression methods, and practical scenarios
stancePerspectives.urban_female: Urban female perspective focusing on wording details, emotional expression, and social issue sensitivities
stancePerspectives.rational_analyst: Rational analysis perspective focusing on logical structure, information accuracy, and technical details
stancePerspectives.public_opinion: General user perspective easily influenced by public discussion atmosphere and comment section emotions
stancePerspectives.independent_thinker: Independent thinking perspective emphasizing individual expression, value consistency, and authentic feelings
stancePerspectives.commercial_observer: Commercial observation perspective focusing on commercial expression, marketing language, and consumption authenticity
stancePerspectives.family_tradition: Traditional culture perspective focusing on family values, intergenerational relations, and traditional value expression
stancePerspectives.community_core: Core player perspective familiar with vertical community culture, focusing on community expression habits and atmosphere
